diagrams.title: File Descriptor Passing Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between two processes, Unix domain socket, and kernel structures during FD passing
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how file descriptors are transferred between process address spaces while both point to the same kernel resource

diagrams.title: cmsghdr Structure Layout
diagrams.description: Memory layout of control message header used to pass file descriptors
diagrams.purpose: Understand the structure needed to package file descriptors in control messages for sendmsg()/recvmsg()

diagrams.title: File Descriptor Table After Passing
diagrams.description: Shows process file descriptor tables and shared open file description after passing
elements: Process A FD table with FD 5 pointing to Open File X
Process B FD table with FD 3 pointing to Open File X
Kernel Open File Table entry X
File offset, access mode, flags shared
Each process must close their own FD
diagrams.purpose: Understand that passing creates independent FDs to the same underlying file

visuals.title: Passing File Descriptors
visuals.description: Visual diagram for passing file descriptors
